title: Locate folded attention KV reducer physical boundary
motivation: The folded reducer frontier showed ppc2 is feasible at 50% utilization for p8 and p16, but ppc2 fails at 60% and ppc4 fails at 50% and 60%. The exact feasibility boundary is still unclear.
hypothesis: The boundary is controlled by a combination of presented partials_per_cycle and physical packing stress. ppc1 should be more robust but slower, ppc2 should have a utilization threshold between 50% and 60%, and ppc4 may require materially lower utilization or a different macro interface.

expected_behavior.functional: All ppc values implement exact folded reduction over a fixed group of partials.
expected_behavior.timing_model: Reducer latency is partials / partials_per_cycle accepted chunks.
expected_behavior.physical_model: Input pins scale with partials_per_cycle while accumulator/output pins remain fixed by lanes and value/stat widths.

risks: If ppc4 remains infeasible at low utilization, the limiting factor may be macro pin topology or output/accumulator structure rather than only core packing.
The scheduler must charge the ppc1/ppc2/ppc4 cycle difference before using the measured PPA in Llama7B schedule estimates.
